rationale: This structure divides the field into three core areas: plant production, animal production, and post-harvest/food systems. Plant production is split into 'Agronomy & Crop Science' (field crops), 'Horticulture' (specialty crops), and 'Plant Protection' (pests/disease), reflecting major divisions in research and practice. Animal production is split into terrestrial ('Animal Science') and aquatic ('Aquaculture & Fisheries'). The post-harvest side is covered by 'Food Science & Technology' (processing) and 'Nutrition Science' (health impact). Cross-cutting disciplines like 'Agricultural Engineering', 'Agricultural Economics', and the systems-focused 'Agroecology' are included as distinct, practitioner-recognized fields. This 12-category structure provides comprehensive coverage without becoming overly granular, ensuring most papers find a clear primary home.

subspecialties.name: Agronomy & Crop Science
subspecialties.tagline: Field crops, soil management, and plant breeding for food and fiber.
explicitly_excludes: pest and disease management → Plant Protection

subspecialties.name: Food Science & Technology
subspecialties.tagline: The chemistry, microbiology, and engineering of food processing.
explicitly_excludes: human nutrition and metabolism → Nutrition Science

subspecialties.name: Animal Science
subspecialties.tagline: Livestock production, breeding, nutrition, and welfare.
explicitly_excludes: companion animal medicine → Veterinary Medicine (separate L1)

subspecialties.name: Nutrition Science
subspecialties.tagline: How food and nutrients affect human health and disease.

subspecialties.name: Plant Protection
subspecialties.tagline: Managing pests, diseases, and weeds that affect plant health.
explicitly_excludes: crop breeding for resistance → Agronomy & Crop Science

subspecialties.name: Horticulture
subspecialties.tagline: Cultivation of fruits, vegetables, nuts, and ornamental plants.
explicitly_excludes: field crops like wheat and corn → Agronomy & Crop Science

subspecialties.name: Agricultural Economics
subspecialties.tagline: The business of food, agriculture, and natural resources.

subspecialties.name: Aquaculture & Fisheries
subspecialties.tagline: Farming and management of fish, shellfish, and other aquatic organisms.

subspecialties.name: Agricultural Engineering
subspecialties.tagline: Technology and machinery for farming, irrigation, and food processing.

subspecialties.name: Agroecology & Sustainable Agriculture
subspecialties.tagline: Ecological principles for sustainable and resilient food systems.
explicitly_excludes: conventional crop management → Agronomy & Crop Science

subspecialties.name: Forestry & Agroforestry
subspecialties.tagline: Management of forests and integrated tree-crop-livestock systems.

subspecialties.name: Soil Science
subspecialties.tagline: The study of soil as a natural resource on the Earth's surface.
explicitly_excludes: routine soil testing for crops → Agronomy & Crop Science

uncovered_concerns: Veterinary Science is a large field; we've included livestock/production animal health in 'Animal Science' to distinguish it from a potential top-level 'Veterinary Medicine' category focused on companion animals.

Agricultural Biotechnology is a tool used across many subspecialties (e.g., breeding, food processing) rather than a distinct category here. Papers on fundamental biotech methods belong in Biology or Biotechnology.
